title: 1.1: Reports HTTP status codes returned for each operation such as bucket creation, upload, or listing (e.g., HTTP 200 OK)
1.2: Reports response headers returned for bucket and object operations such as Location, ETag, Content-Type, X-Amz-Request-Id, or Server
1.3: Reports server-side log messages for bucket operations such as s3.PutBucket or s3.ListObjectsV2 log entries with timestamps (e.g., 2026-01-07T08:02:36.431)
1.4: Identifies filesystem evidence of data storage such as bucket directories or object metadata files (e.g., /tmp/minio-data/bucket-name/object/xl.meta)
1.5: Reports the response body structure when listing objects in a bucket such as XML format containing object keys, sizes, timestamps, or ETags
1.6: Reports evidence that bucket or objects persist after server restart (e.g., retrieval of previously uploaded objects returning HTTP 200 OK after restart)
1.7: Reports that bucket creation returns an empty response body (e.g., Content-Length: 0)
1.8: Reports server-side log messages indicating data write operations such as s3.PutObject log entries
1.9: Reports server-side log entries showing when operations complete such as [RESPONSE] markers or TTFB metrics (e.g., TTFB 15.371ms)
1.10: Reports what access or authorization error messages appear when credentials are missing such as HTTP 403 Forbidden with error codes like AccessDenied or InvalidAccessKeyId
1.11: Reports the download response including HTTP status code (e.g., HTTP 200 OK) and the actual object content in the response body
1.12: Reports server-side log messages indicating data read operations such as s3.GetObject log entries showing download activity
1.13: States how the system processes requests based on request details observed in logs such as HTTP method, path, client IP, or request headers
1.14: Reports the MinIO server startup message with listening endpoint URLs (e.g., API on port 9000, WebUI on port 9001)
1.15: Reports that at least two different objects were uploaded to the bucket such as text files, JSON files, or objects with different names
1.16: Reports that object upload returns an empty response body (e.g., Content-Length: 0) with ETag header
